nameMeanings.name: Poges
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from a place called Potes in Cantabria.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

en.lifeSummary: Humphrey Poges was born about 1220, in Stoke Poges, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Unknown Poges and Unknown. He married Mrs Pugeys about 1260, in England, United Kingdom. He died about 1278, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60.

ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey Poges(은)는 대략 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 스토크 포그스에서 Unknown Poges(와)과 Unknown의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1260년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Mrs Pugeys(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 대략 1278년 영국 잉글랜드에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
